---
title: "Talks About the Laws We Live Under, or, at Langley Night School"
sort_title: "Talks About the Laws We Live Under, or, at Langley Night School"
year: 1882
slug: "talks about the laws we live under, or, Langley night school"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
---


*1882*



**[Click here to reach the online text of](http://ia351419.us.archive.org/1/items/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog/)**[*Talks about the Laws we live under, or, at Langley night school*](http://ia351419.us.archive.org/1/items/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog/)

**[Click here to download a PDF file of](http://ia351419.us.archive.org/1/items/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog.pdf)**[*Talks about the laws*](http://ia351419.us.archive.org/1/items/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog/talksaboutlawsw00yonggoog.pdf)******  
(scan of a Oxford Bodleian Library copy)**  
*(note - large file - over 7 Mb)*

#### From Yonge's Preface toTalks about the Laws we live under(preface dated 1881)

In our dread of being accused of influencing on behalf of one party or the other, we are, as it appears to me, leaving our population utterly uninstructed in their duties as citizens, and then we blame them for their misuse of rights they have never been taught to value at their true worth. I have endeavoured as far as possible to avoid all tokens of political bias, that no fear of inculcating party views may interfere with the use of the book.
